name: Ancient music
shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for ancient music.

name: Medieval music
shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for medieval music.

name: Renaissance music
shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for renaissance music.

name: Baroque
shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for baroque.

name: Classical period
shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for classical period.

name: Romanticism
shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for romanticism.

name: Modernism
shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for modernism.

name: Post-war music
shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for post-war music.

name: Digital music age
shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for digital music age.

name: Folk music
shortDescription: Community-based music traditions transmitted through local practice and memory.

name: Western classical music
shortDescription: Art-music tradition associated with notation, institutions and long-form composition.

name: Blues
shortDescription: African American musical form centered on expressive vocal style, blue notes and repeated harmonic patterns.

name: Gospel
shortDescription: Sacred vocal tradition rooted in Christian worship, communal singing and expressive call-and-response.

name: Jazz
shortDescription: Improvisatory music tradition shaped by swing, blues, ensemble interaction and harmonic exploration.

name: Country
shortDescription: Popular music tradition drawing on folk ballads, rural storytelling and string-band practices.

name: Rhythm and blues
shortDescription: Post-war Black popular music emphasizing groove, vocals and dance-oriented rhythm sections.

name: Soul
shortDescription: Vocal-centered popular music combining rhythm and blues, gospel intensity and secular expression.

name: Funk
shortDescription: Groove-driven music emphasizing syncopated bass, drums, riffs and collective rhythmic interlock.

name: Rock and roll
shortDescription: 1950s popular music formed from rhythm and blues, blues, country and youth culture.

name: Rock
shortDescription: Amplified popular music tradition built around drums, bass, guitar, vocals and album/song forms.

name: Punk
shortDescription: Raw, fast and often anti-establishment rock movement emphasizing directness and DIY practice.

name: Post-punk
shortDescription: Experimental aftermath of punk that expanded rock with electronics, dub, art-school ideas and new textures.

name: Disco
shortDescription: Dance music culture and production style built around steady pulse, orchestration and club playback.

name: House
shortDescription: Electronic dance music developed around four-on-the-floor rhythms, DJs, drum machines and clubs.

name: Techno
shortDescription: Electronic dance music associated with machine rhythm, futurism and synthesized sound design.

name: Hip hop
shortDescription: Cultural and musical form built around MCing, DJing, beats, sampling and urban expression.

name: Reggae
shortDescription: Jamaican popular music marked by offbeat rhythm, bass weight, songcraft and sound-system culture.

name: Dub
shortDescription: Studio-centered Jamaican practice using remixing, echo, reverb, bass and instrumental versions.

name: Dancehall
shortDescription: Jamaican music culture emphasizing deejay vocals, digital riddims and sound-system performance.

name: Pop
shortDescription: Broad popular song tradition organized around hooks, mass media circulation and changing production styles.

name: Synthpop
shortDescription: Pop music centered on synthesizers, drum machines, sequencers and electronic textures.

name: Electronic music
shortDescription: Music made with electronic sound generation, processing, sequencing or computer-based production.

name: Ambient
shortDescription: Music focused on atmosphere, texture, space and sustained listening environments.

name: Minimalism
shortDescription: Compositional movement using repetition, gradual process and reduced musical materials.

name: Afrobeat
shortDescription: West African popular music combining groove, horns, extended forms and political expression.

name: Latin music
shortDescription: Broad set of Latin American and diasporic musical traditions, rhythms and popular forms.

name: Nordic folk music
shortDescription: Folk traditions from Nordic regions, including dance forms, fiddle practices and local song heritage.

name: Norwegian pop
shortDescription: Norwegian popular music tradition shaped by local language, international pop and Nordic production contexts.

name: Film music
shortDescription: Music composed, selected or produced to support cinematic narrative, mood and audiovisual timing.

name: Game music
shortDescription: Interactive and linear music created for video games, gameplay states and digital worlds.

name: Rhythm
shortDescription: Organization of musical time through duration, pulse, accent and pattern.

name: Syncopation
shortDescription: Rhythmic displacement that stresses off-beats or unexpected parts of the meter.

name: Groove
shortDescription: Felt rhythmic momentum created by repeated patterns, timing and ensemble interaction.

name: Polyrhythm
shortDescription: Simultaneous use of contrasting rhythmic patterns or meters.

name: Call and response
shortDescription: Responsive musical structure where one phrase is answered by another.

name: Melody
shortDescription: Ordered sequence of pitches perceived as a musical line.

name: Harmony
shortDescription: Combination and progression of pitches or chords sounding together.

name: Tonality
shortDescription: Pitch organization around a central key or tonic.

name: Modality
shortDescription: Pitch organization using modes or scalar frameworks beyond major/minor tonality.

name: Drone
shortDescription: Sustained or repeated pitch foundation used as a tonal reference.

name: Improvisation
shortDescription: Real-time musical creation within stylistic, social or formal constraints.

name: Sampling
shortDescription: Use of recorded sound fragments as material in a new production.

name: Loop
shortDescription: Repeated audio or musical pattern used as a structural building block.

name: Timbre
shortDescription: Perceived sound color that distinguishes instruments, voices and textures.

name: Overtones
shortDescription: Frequencies above a fundamental that shape tone color and resonance.

name: Resonance
shortDescription: Amplification or reinforcement of vibration in bodies, rooms or instruments.

name: Distortion
shortDescription: Sound alteration caused by saturation, clipping or nonlinear amplification.

name: Reverb
shortDescription: Persistence of sound reflections that creates a sense of space.

name: Multitrack recording
shortDescription: Recording method that captures and mixes separate sound layers independently.

name: Synthesizer
shortDescription: Electronic instrument or system for generating and shaping sound.

name: Drum machine
shortDescription: Electronic instrument for sequencing and playing percussion sounds.

name: Sound system
shortDescription: Playback and amplification culture centered on DJs, selectors and powerful speakers.

name: Studio as instrument
shortDescription: Production approach where recording, mixing and effects become compositional tools.

name: Ritual music
shortDescription: Music used in ceremonial, sacred or repeated social practices.

name: Dance culture
shortDescription: Social contexts where music is organized around dancing, bodies and shared movement.

name: Urban music culture
shortDescription: City-based musical practices shaped by density, scenes, migration and media.

name: Youth culture
shortDescription: Generational music practices tied to identity, style, rebellion and peer communities.

name: Protest music
shortDescription: Music used to voice dissent, solidarity, critique or political mobilization.

name: Migration and music
shortDescription: Musical exchange, memory and hybrid forms shaped by movement of people.

name: Black Atlantic music
shortDescription: Transatlantic Black musical continuum linking African diasporic histories and modern popular forms.

name: Club culture
shortDescription: Nightlife and DJ-centered spaces where dance music scenes, taste and technology develop.

name: Music video culture
shortDescription: Audiovisual pop culture shaped by television, video clips, image and performance.

name: Internet music culture
shortDescription: Networked music circulation shaped by platforms, memes, communities and digital release practices.
